Fix: NativeMujocoViewer does not display sim.model.qpos0 changes (#760)

* Add 'qpos0' to visual fields set

NativeMujocoViewer does not display sim.model.qpos0 changes.

When dr.joint_default_pos (alias dr.qpos0) randomizes qpos0 per environment, the GPU sim.model.qpos0 gets per-world values. However, the CPU MjModel used by the native viewer never receives these changes because qpos0 is filtered out by _VISUAL_FIELDS.

The minimal fix would be to add "qpos0" to the set of fields synced in _sync_model_fields.

commit.message:
chore: add *.log to .gitignore to prevent test log file commits (#1275)

## Problem

An RSpec log file (`rspec_full_run_20251215_184759.log`) was
accidentally committed to the repo root on Dec 15 2025 and flagged by
GitGuardian (Dec 16 2025) as containing a Generic High Entropy Secret.

The file was removed in commit 87ca87b but remains in git history.

## Root Cause

`.gitignore` had `/log` (covers the `log/` directory) but not `*.log` at
the repo root, so a log file saved to the root was not excluded.

## Fix

Add `*.log` to `.gitignore` to prevent future accidental commits of test
log output files.

## Note on History

The original commit is still in git history. The secret appears to be a
test-generated token (not a production credential), so risk is low. If
desired, `git filter-repo` can be used to scrub the history.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add kernel-mode QUIC server backend (msquic-km) (#3)

* feat: add kernel-mode QUIC server backend (msquic-km)

Add a WDM kernel driver (winquicecho_km.sys) that calls the msquic.sys
kernel-mode API — the same API surface used by http.sys and SMB server —
to implement a datagram echo server.

Components:
- Kernel driver (src/backends/msquic_km/driver/winquicecho_km.c):
  WDM driver that opens MsQuicOpenVersion from msquic.sys, configures
  Schannel credentials, starts a QUIC listener, and echoes datagrams
  back in the connection callback. Controlled via IOCTLs through
  \\.\WinQuicEcho device.
- Shared IOCTL header (src/backends/msquic_km/ioctl.h):
  IOCTL codes and shared structs for START/STOP/GET_STATS.
- User-mode backend (src/backends/msquic_km/msquic_km_backend.cpp):
  Implements quic_backend interface; opens device handle, sends IOCTLs,
  polls stats. Registered as 'msquic-km' in the backend factory.
- Build/install scripts (scripts/build-km-driver.ps1,
  scripts/install-km-driver.ps1): Build with WDK toolchain, install
  as kernel service with msquic dependency.
- INF file for driver installation.

Usage: echo_server.exe --backend msquic-km --cert-hash <thumbprint>

Tested end-to-end on Windows Server 2025 VM: 14,321 echo roundtrips
at ~4,800 RPS with 2 connections, no crashes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alanjo@microsoft.com>

* ci: add kernel-mode QUIC echo integration test

Add a CI job that builds the winquicecho_km.sys driver, signs it with
an ephemeral test certificate, installs it as a kernel service, and
runs the echo roundtrip integration test against the msquic-km backend.

Changes:
- tests/integration/echo-roundtrip.ps1: Add --Backend parameter
  (default: msquic). When msquic-km, uses LocalMachine\My cert store
  for kernel-mode Schannel compatibility.
- .github/workflows/reusable-test-km.yml: New reusable workflow that
  downloads WDK/SDK NuGet packages, MsQuic kernel headers, compiles
  and links the driver, signs and installs it, then runs the test.
  Skips gracefully if msquic.sys is absent or driver fails to load.
- .github/workflows/cicd.yml: Add test-km job parallel to test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alanjo@microsoft.com>

* fix(ci): add MSVC CRT include path for kernel driver build

The WDK's wdm.h includes excpt.h from the MSVC CRT. When compiling
with cl /kernel, include paths are not auto-discovered, so the MSVC
include directory must be passed explicitly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alanjo@microsoft.com>

* fix(ci): move kernel driver build to build step

Build the kernel driver alongside user-mode binaries in
reusable-build.yml instead of in the test workflow. This:
- Uses MsQuic headers from FetchContent (already fetched by CMake)
- Includes the UCRT headers that were missing (ctype.h)
- Uploads winquicecho_km.sys as part of the build artifact
- Simplifies reusable-test-km.yml to just sign/install/test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alanjo@microsoft.com>

* fix(ci): correct artifact download paths for multi-path upload

upload-artifact with multiple paths strips the common ancestor (build/)
so downloaded files are at downloaded_build/Release/ and downloaded_build/km/
rather than downloaded_build/build/Release/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alanjo@microsoft.com>

* fix: address review comments on kernel-mode backend

- Fix DGRAM_SEND_CTX leak during shutdown: handle
  DATAGRAM_SEND_STATE_CHANGED even when Running==0
- Fix ConnectionSetConfiguration failure: move ActiveConnections
  increment before the call so SHUTDOWN_COMPLETE can clean up
- Fix StopEchoServer race: call RegistrationShutdown to trigger
  graceful connection close before tearing down MsQuic API table
- Fix run_server exception safety: call stop_server in catch block
  so kernel server is not left running on error
- Fix stop_server: check DeviceIoControl return value and log errors
- Fix get_stats: validate bytes_returned matches expected struct size
- Remove unused SymlinkCreated variable in DriverEntry
- Add missing <mutex> and <cctype> includes in msquic_km_backend.cpp
- Add admin elevation check in echo-roundtrip.ps1 for LocalMachine
  cert store access (msquic-km backend)
- Add clarifying comment on IoCreateDevice security descriptor

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alan Jowett <alanjo@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
Support both `agentgateway.dev/mcp` and `kgateway.dev/mcp` as appProtocol values (#1168)

MCP service discovery only recognized `kgateway.dev/mcp` /
`kgateway.dev/mcp-sse` as valid `appProtocol` values, silently ignoring
services annotated with `agentgateway.dev/mcp`.

## Changes

- **`mcp.go`**: Promote `agentgateway.dev/mcp` and
`agentgateway.dev/mcp-sse` to primary constants; retain `kgateway.dev/*`
variants as legacy aliases
- **`translate.go`**: Extend protocol filter and `toMCPProtocol` switch
to accept both constant families, mapping identically to
`STREAMABLE_HTTP` / `SSE`

Both forms now work:

```yaml
ports:
  - appProtocol: agentgateway.dev/mcp   # preferred
  - appProtocol: kgateway.dev/mcp       # still supported (backwards compat)
```

feat: incremental log fetching with range support and append-on-expire caching (#13)

* feat: incremental log fetching with range support and append-on-expire caching

Add startLine/endLine parameters to IAzdoApiClient.GetBuildLogAsync for
AzDO REST API range requests. Implement dual-key freshness caching in
CachingAzdoApiClient that delta-fetches only new lines when cached content
goes stale (15s for in-progress builds, 4h for completed). Add server-side
tail optimization in AzdoService using lineCount metadata with 2x threshold.

- D-1: API range support (0-indexed startLine/endLine query params)
- D-2: Hybrid cache (full cached → extract range; uncached range → pass-through)
- D-3: Tail optimization (skip metadata call when lineCount <= tailLines * 2)
- D-5: Line indexing (0-based at API, 1-based at service layer)
- D-6: Append-on-expire with dual-key freshness markers

commit.message:
feat: TLS termination via direct SslStream on Kestrel

Bypass Kestrel's UseHttps() (requires DI/IServiceProvider) by adding
a connection middleware that wraps the raw TCP transport in SslStream.
Pure BCL — no external libraries, no DI container.

- TlsConnectionMiddleware: SslStream handshake at connection layer,
  replaces Kestrel transport with PipeReader/PipeWriter over SslStream
- DuplexStream: bridges PipeReader.AsStream + PipeWriter.AsStream
  into a single bidirectional Stream for SslStream
- Dockerfile.prebuilt: switched from dotnet chiseled image to ubuntu:24.04
  with libssl3 (AOT binary is self-contained, doesn't need runtime-deps)
- docs/architecture/hosting.md: full hosting model documentation
- Cert managed by cert-manager (Let's Encrypt DNS-01 via Azure DNS)
- HTTP/1.1 over TLS 1.2+1.3 on port 5233, H2 on cleartext port 5232

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add terminal `Else` overloads for `FailOr<T>` and `Task<FailOr<T>>` (#8)

[Evaluation] Fix empty alt text image crash in evaluation pipeline (ICM 756728740) (#45587)

* Fix empty alt text image crash in evaluation pipeline (ICM 756728740)

PR #45522 fixed InvalidInputError for unresolvable relative image paths
from Document Intelligence, but IMAGE_URL_PARSING_PATTERN required at
least one character in the alt text group ([^\]]+). Markdown like
crashed because the regex didn't match.

Changes:
- Changed [^\]]+ to [^\]]* in IMAGE_URL_PARSING_PATTERN to accept
  empty alt text, which is valid markdown syntax
- Added defense-in-depth fallback: any markdown image syntax that still
  can't be parsed is returned as plain text instead of crashing

commit.message:
refactor: eliminate file name stuttering violations (SL-008)

Renamed 19 stuttering files across internal/ packages to follow Go
naming conventions:

Wayland packages:
- client/client.go → client/connection.go
- datadevice/datadevice.go → datadevice/manager.go
- dmabuf/dmabuf.go → dmabuf/protocol.go
- input/input.go → input/seat.go
- output/output.go → output/protocol.go
- shm/shm.go → shm/protocol.go
- socket/socket.go → socket/connection.go
- wire/wire.go → wire/protocol.go
- xdg/xdg.go → xdg/wmbase.go

X11 packages:
- client/client.go → client/connection.go
- dpi/dpi.go → dpi/detector.go
- dri3/dri3.go → dri3/extension.go
- events/events.go → events/types.go
- gc/gc.go → gc/ops.go
- present/present.go → present/extension.go
- selection/selection.go → selection/manager.go
- shm/shm.go → shm/extension.go
- wire/wire.go → wire/protocol.go

Render packages:
- present/present.go → present/interface.go

New names are descriptive and indicate file purpose:
- protocol.go: Wire protocol implementations
- connection.go: Connection/client types
- manager.go: Manager types
- interface.go: Interface definitions
- extension.go: X11 protocol extensions
- Others: Specific type/function names (detector, ops, seat, wmbase, types)

Impact:
- Resolved all 30 file-name stuttering violations
- Improved naming gate score from 0.99 toward 1.0
- All tests pass (19 affected packages verified)
- Zero regressions in complexity, duplication, or documentation

Closes: SL-008 (AUDIT.md Phase 5)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(copilot): add Windows platform guard to _fix_pipe_blocking_mode() (#26)

* fix(copilot): add Windows platform guard to _fix_pipe_blocking_mode()

_fix_pipe_blocking_mode() unconditionally imports fcntl, a Unix-only
module, causing ModuleNotFoundError on Windows. O_NONBLOCK does not
exist on Windows and pipes are always blocking, so the method is a
no-op on that platform.

Add sys.platform == 'win32' early return before the fcntl import.

Adds 2 tests:
- test_skips_on_windows: verifies no-op on win32
- test_runs_on_unix: verifies the method proceeds past the guard

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent EVM memory size overflow crash for extreme memory requests (#9887)

fix: prevent EVM memory size overflow crash for large memory requests

The memory validation in CheckMemoryAccessViolation was checking against
long.MaxValue, but .NET arrays are limited to int.MaxValue. When memory
requests exceeded int.MaxValue but were less than long.MaxValue, the
word-aligned size calculation would overflow when cast to int, causing
ArrayPool.Rent() to allocate an incorrectly sized array and subsequent
Array.Copy operations to crash.

This fix changes the limit from long.MaxValue to (int.MaxValue - WordSize + 1)
to ensure that after word alignment, the resulting size still fits in int.

Root cause:
- Memory size validation checked: totalSize > long.MaxValue
- But .NET arrays require: (int)Size to be valid
- Word alignment adds up to 31 bytes: Size = totalSize + (32 - totalSize % 32)
- When totalSize > int.MaxValue - 31, (int)Size overflows

Example crash scenario:
1. Contract requests 4GB (0xffffffff) memory via DELEGATECALL
2. Validation passes (4GB < long.MaxValue)
3. Word-aligned Size = 0x100000000 (exceeds int.MaxValue)
4. (int)Size = 0 (overflow)
5. ArrayPool.Rent(0) returns tiny array
6. Array.Copy crashes with "Destination array was not long enough"

* fix: only touch coinbase after successful transaction in state tests (#9865)

* fix: only touch coinbase after successful transaction in state tests

When a transaction fails validation (e.g., insufficient balance), the
state test runner should not modify state at all. Previously, the
coinbase account was created in InitializeTestState before transaction
execution, causing state root divergence from geth when transactions
failed validation.

This fix moves coinbase creation to after successful transaction
execution, matching geth's behavior:
- Only touch coinbase when txResult == Ok
- When validation fails, state remains unchanged from pre-state

This was a consensus bug in the test framework (not production code)
introduced in PR #9225.

Co-authored-by: rubo <rubo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(sync): use INetworkConfig constructor for SyncPeerPool DI registration (#10158)

* fix(sync): use INetworkConfig constructor for SyncPeerPool DI registration

SyncPeerPool was ignoring the configured MaxActivePeers value and always
using a hardcoded default of 100 peers.

Root cause: SyncPeerPool has two constructors:
1. One taking INetworkConfig that reads ActivePeersMaxCount from config
2. One with default parameters (peersMaxCount = 100)

The DI registration `.AddSingleton<SyncPeerPool>()` allowed Autofac to
pick a constructor automatically. Autofac selected the constructor with
default parameters, completely bypassing INetworkConfig. This caused
SyncPeerPool.PeerMaxCount to always be 100 regardless of the configured
Network.MaxActivePeers value.

Fix: Use explicit factory registration that specifies INetworkConfig as
a dependency, ensuring the correct constructor is called with config values.

* Optimize progress tracking with active level and startup delay

Improvements:
- Add 1 second startup delay before logging to prevent early high
  values from getting stuck in _maxReportedProgress
- Only track the deepest level with >5% coverage (active level)
- Stop incrementing counts for shallower levels once deeper level
  has significant coverage
- This ensures progress never shows less than 5% and provides
  more accurate granularity

Technical changes:
- Add _activeLevel field to track current deepest significant level
- Add _startTime field and skip logging for first second
- Only increment seen counts at active level or deeper
- Automatically promote to deeper level when >5% coverage reached

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Simplify progress tracking to only use level 3 with leaf estimation

Changed to a much simpler approach as requested:
- Only track progress at level 3 (4 nibbles = 65536 possible nodes)
- For nodes at depth 4: increment count by 1
- For LEAF nodes at shallower depths: estimate coverage
  - Depth 1: covers 16^3 = 4096 level-3 nodes
  - Depth 2: covers 16^2 = 256 level-3 nodes
  - Depth 3: covers 16^1 = 16 level-3 nodes
- Non-leaf nodes at shallow depths: don't count (will be covered by deeper nodes)
- Keep 1 second startup delay to prevent early high percentages

This assumes the top of the tree is dense and provides accurate
progress estimation based on actual trie structure.

Updated tests to mark nodes as leaves where appropriate.

commit.message:
Sprint 31: Migrate Key.Char from char to Text.Rune

- Key.Char now holds Text.Rune instead of char, enabling supplementary
  Unicode characters (emoji, CJK ext-B, etc.) in keyboard input
- Added [<AutoOpen>] module InputHelpers with (|KeyChar|_|) active pattern
  for ergonomic pattern matching (Rune can't appear as a literal in patterns)
- Recording format change: char:X -> char:<scalar_int> for full Unicode range
- TextInput cursor advances by r.ToString().Length (fixes emoji off-by-one)
- Surrogate pair reassembly in Detect.fs now emits assembled Rune directly
- All consuming files updated: System.Text.Rune for construction,
  KeyChar 'x' active pattern for pattern matching (avoids Element.Text shadowing)
- 1,648 tests pass
